days.title: Button polish — Pre-Onboarding Meets & Feedback Analytics
days.narrative: Pre-Onboarding Meets and Feedback Analytics had broken button styling — gradient blobs without padding or radius because the base btn class was missing and fb-chip/fb-tab-btn CSS was never defined. I restored proper button foundations, refactored the meet scheduler modal to match the batch-assignment modal pattern, and added full filter-chip, tab-toggle, and export-dropdown styles. Scheduling meets and filtering feedback now feels consistent with the rest of the L&D console.
milestones: Pre-Onboarding Meets buttons match app design system
Feedback Analytics filter chips, tabs, and export dropdown styled

days.title: L&D console upgrade — charts, AI helper, unified batch composer
days.narrative: The L&D console finally feels like a command center. Batch Composer merges AI segregation and approve/publish into two tabs. Ops Dashboard gets Three.js visualizations and Recharts fed by live roster data. Recruitment queue seed migration restores 18 recruits when localStorage goes stale. Effectiveness Loop and Feedback Analytics show real data with dark-theme charts and working CSV/Excel/PDF exports. Report Generator PDF and Excel exports work. AI Helper uses an MCP agent for natural-language roster queries with export. Maratype display font on heroes and welcome messages. Skill Grove is replaced by Training Universe — a 3D planet map with five stages; Deployment and Onboarding are one combined final planet, locked until internship completes. Completing a stage zooms the camera out to overview before the rocket flies to the next planet. Chest reward modal layout fixed so skill text no longer overlaps the heading. Maratype bold only on headings; Noto Sans for body text. Pre-onboarding meets now use real Google Calendar OAuth — L&D connects Gmail once, schedules create live Meet links, SMTP invites trainer and batch, and GPT-5 analyzes transcripts after session end. Google Cloud credentials configured; API rebuilt and restarted on port 3003.
milestones: Batch Composer two-tab workflow (segregation + approve)
Ops Dashboard Three.js + live data charts
AI Helper with MCP agent and exports
Working PDF/Excel exports across analytics and reports
Skill Grove replaced with Training Universe — five planets; Deployment & Onboarding combined as final locked stage
Stage completion zoom-out then rocket flight transition
Maratype headings only; Noto Sans body in Training Universe
Google Calendar OAuth for real Meet links + GPT-5 session analysis pipeline

days.title: Landing music mute actually mutes
days.narrative: The landing page mute button finally does what it says. Two audio sources were fighting — the galaxy iframe was auto-playing its own ambient track while the top-bar toggle only controlled a separate parent player, so Mute never silenced what you were actually hearing. I disabled autoplay in embed mode, wired the toggle to a single DOM audio element, and synced mute state to the iframe via postMessage so one click stops everything. Then the Stellardrone MP3 on Free Music Archive never loads on our network — DNS can't resolve the CDN — so play() always failed and the retry toast covered the mute button. I swapped in a self-contained Web Audio ambient synth (no external files), removed that toast, and moved login toasts to the bottom of the screen. Magic link sign-in was broken for two reasons: Vite was proxying /api to port 3001 (Sanctuary, not NextSteps), and the API never sent email. I wired magic-link + OTP, pointed the dev proxy at nextsteps-api on 3003, and restored galaxy interactivity. The recruitment queue got add-recruit and assign-to-batch — but the assign button silently failed because the store re-read stale localStorage. I aligned eligibility, synced seat counts, and wired the button to live React state so assignments stick on the first click. Onboarding mail is real now — L&D drafts role, location, date, and attachment; the API sends a branded welcome via SMTP. Sign-in rules match the roster: batch-assigned trainees can magic-link in; queue-only recruits get a clear wait message. Your face follows you through the Nebula: names title-case everywhere, and avatars use Microsoft Graph for SSO or Gmail profile photos for personal email — sidebar, passport, and hyperspeed lanyard all show the real you.
milestones: Landing music mute/unmute controls all audible sources
Landing ambient music works without external CDN
Magic link + OTP auth with dev fallback
Recruitment queue assign-to-batch works end-to-end
Real profile photos in sidebar, passport, and hyperspeed lanyard

days.title: 3D grove, AI buddy, feedback analytics — full nebula stack live
days.narrative: Today NextSteps became a place you walk into. The galaxy backdrop is fully interactive — drag, zoom, parallax — while every UI button still works, thanks to a careful pointer-events architecture. Dark mode is locked in, theme toggle is gone, music mutes properly. The Skill Grove is a full-page 3D tree with no chrome: branches glow violet for completed skills, amber for in-progress, fruits bloom on achievements. L&D has two new power tools: a 50-record Trainee Roster with side-panel profiles and one-click onboarding email automation, and a Feedback Analytics dashboard unifying all Maverick-to-trainer and manager-to-Maverick feedback with filterable tables, ranked analytics, and CSV export. The AI Learning Buddy now runs on real Azure OpenAI (gpt-5), as does transcript analysis. Every component got proper nebula-themed CSS: glowing AI chat, skill-label chips, feedback side-panel. The hyperspeed splash shows your Graph API photo, name, and employee ID on a floating lanyard badge.
milestones: 3D Skill Grove full-page canvas — branches=levels, fruits=achievements, zero UI chrome
FeedbackAnalytics page — unified L&D feedback view with analytics, flags, and CSV export
AI Learning Buddy and transcript analysis wired to Azure OpenAI gpt-5
NebulaBadge overlay on hyperspeed splash shows Graph API identity
TraineeRoster with onboarding email auto-provisioning and side-panel profiles
challenges: SkillTree main-content padding was cropping the 3D canvas — fixed with :has() override
days.lookingAhead: Connect magic link email send end-to-end with actual SMTP; verify SSO role routing with real Azure AD groups.

days.title: Maverick Nebula — welcome shipped, privacy hardened
days.narrative: ALSAA-63 heartbeat 2: resumed after adapter-limit failure, cleared missing-disposition gate. Wave 2 now fully done (ALSAA-61 privacy + ALSAA-62 P7 analytics). Critical path is ALSAA-57 close-out + stalled P2/P4/P5 (ALSAA-14/16/17). Posted delta rollup on monitor; parent comment still 409 (concurrent ALSAA-54 checkout). Heartbeat 1 established CoS monitor after CEO handoff. Closed ALSAA-61/62/58/59, rebuilt Maverick Nebula with SSO.

days.title: Strategy complete + ADO ladder plan — Build delegation
days.narrative: Strategy closed with the epic roadmap v1.0.0 (ALSAA-10): ten epics, P0–P6 mapped to ALSAA-12–18. Scrum Master delivered ALSAA-11 planning — seven Friday Features (0.1.0–0.7.0), Paperclip→ADO story mirror, and a starter routing matrix under Lab AI Tools; CEO approval gates the actual board sync. Build is in flight: P4 session intelligence split into specialist tasks, P2 pulse triaged for privacy and scaffold gaps (ALSAA-25→27), P5 manager/auth migration delegated (ALSAA-28/30/32/33). The program now has both an engineering roadmap and a board execution blueprint.

days.title: Brand shell polish + Magic Bento interactions on real cards
days.narrative: The product wears one deliberate visual story (indigo, violet, amber; Hyperspeed; Noto Sans everywhere; DiceBear Personas avatars; horizontal Passport lanyard; dark-friendly HQ cards; sidebar scrollbar and Sign out contrast fixes). I then wired React Bits–style Magic Bento for production: GSAP spotlight and glow across Layout main, AppMagicCard on real card/stat/login/chat surfaces, motion wrappers preserved. A codemod bug matching card inside card-header taught tighter token rules; I dropped a phantom Dashboard bento import for a trainer toolkit strip that builds. Next I'd lazy-load chart-heavy routes if perf feedback says so.
milestones: Tri-color palette wired through CSS, charts, and hyperspeedEffectColors()
Global Noto Sans typography (--font-app); removed Inter / Space Grotesk overrides
DiceBear Personas avatars (seed = Maverick ID) across shell and Passport
Maverick Passport as full-page horizontal lanyard credential card
Dark-theme Mission HQ accent cards + theme-aware XP ring
Sidebar shell polish: solid wordmark + inset nav accent + neutral scrollbar
Magic Bento (GSAP): global spotlight + AppMagicCard on dashboards, forms, login, AI Buddy
challenges: Keeping semantic UI (success vs risk) readable with only three hues
Sidebar scrollbar / glow reading as a purple seam beside content
Codemod false positives: word-boundary card matched card-header / passport-*-card class names
days.lookingAhead: Optional lazy-loading for chart-heavy pages if perf feedback comes in.
